meaning: n.<英> (尤指伦敦街头)卖艺人(歌手或奏乐者)
examples: The most common kind of busker plays a guitar.
A busker is someone who performs music or an act on the street.
I refuse to give money to any busker playing Simon and garfunkel.
Levi's full-length debut, it finds a sweet spot between disjointed pop and busker garage.
Steven Baldock describes himself as the "last Victorian pub pianist" and goes by the stage name of Dr. Busker.
